disclaimer: Snapshot for routing assistants. Prices and model availability change frequently. Always confirm on the provider's official pricing and models pages before relying on costs. OpenAI pricing rows use the Standard tier unless noted.

reasoning_level_scale.none: No meaningful internal reasoning mode; fastest/cheapest tier for trivial tasks.
reasoning_level_scale.low: Light reasoning; good for classification, extraction, short answers.
reasoning_level_scale.medium: General chat and coding; balances cost and quality.
reasoning_level_scale.high: Strong reasoning and long-context work; default for difficult tasks.
reasoning_level_scale.very_high: Frontier quality; use when failure cost is high.
reasoning_level_scale.reasoning_native: Model family optimized for extended chain-of-thought / reasoning tokens (often higher latency and cost).

cross_provider_routing_hints: Match task difficulty to reasoning_level first, then optimize cost within that band.
For tool-heavy agent loops, prefer mid-tier fast models (Sonnet, GPT-5.4-mini, Gemini 2.5 Flash, Grok fast) unless a step fails validation.
Use reasoning_native OpenAI o-series or Grok *-reasoning when explicit multi-step deliberation is required and latency is acceptable.
For multimodal + massive context, compare Gemini (1M+ context on 2.5 Flash) vs Claude Opus/Sonnet 4.6 (1M) vs Grok 4 (2M) vs GPT-5.4 family per current docs.
Check deprecation dates (Claude Haiku 3, Gemini 2.0 family) before baking into long-lived routing.
Provider-specific tool surcharges (xAI web_search, Google grounding) can dominate bill; route tool-light tasks to models without forced grounding.

openai.label: OpenAI
openai.pricing_tiers_note: Public pricing lists Standard, Batch (~50% off many models), Flex, and Priority multipliers. This catalog uses Standard token rows unless a row is labeled otherwise.

models.notes: Default flagship; context pricing note in OpenAI docs for <272K prompt length.
Lower cost GPT-5.4 family; good for drafts and tool-heavy loops.
Cheapest GPT-5.4 tier; classification, simple transforms.
Highest quality/latency tradeoff; use for hardest reasoning or production-critical output.
Strong general model; balance of capability vs 5.4 flagship.
Legacy; prefer GPT-5.x for new projects unless pinned.
Strong multimodal; legacy vs GPT-5 vision workflows.
Dated snapshot id; pin only for reproducibility.
Reasoning-optimized; higher latency/cost; math, planning, hard puzzles.
General reasoning model; good alternative when chain-of-thought style depth needed.
Deep research agent workflow; expect multi-step tool use.
Moderation; priced as free on OpenAI pricing page.

pricing.notes: Prompt caching, batch, and thinking-token pricing: see Anthropic pricing page.

when_to_avoid: High-volume low-complexity traffic where Haiku suffices
models.knowledge_cutoff_notes: See Anthropic models table (reliable vs training cutoff).

models.notes: Prior generation; migrate to 4.6 when possible.
Prior Opus; prefer Opus 4.6 for new work.
Anthropic announced retirement April 19, 2026; migrate to Haiku 4.5.
Cybersecurity defensive workflows (Project Glasswing); not self-serve.

xai.label: xAI
xai.provider_notes: Grok training cutoff Nov 2024 per xAI docs; enable web_search/x_search for current events. Batch API: 50% off token costs for text models (see xAI batch docs).

best_for: X/real-time angle when using xAI search tools
Same tier as reasoning variant without explicit reasoning mode
models.notes: Same token price as reasoning id; pick based on need for reasoning traces.

server_side_tool_surcharges.notes: Token costs still apply; image/video understanding via tools billed as tokens per xAI docs.

models.context_window_notes: Tiered pricing at 200k prompt tokens (see pricing page).

pricing.notes: Output includes thinking tokens; grounding to Search/Maps extra per Google table.

best_for: Vibe coding workflows per Google copy
use_cases: Primary Gemini flagship when available on your billing tier

models.notes: Thinking tokens included in output price.

pricing.notes: Live API: mixed per-token and per-minute audio rates on Google pricing page.
Image output billed as tokens (~$0.045–0.151 per image by resolution per Google).
Text/thinking aligned with Gemini 3.1 Pro rates; image output $120/1M tokens per Google.
Text priced as 2.5 Flash; image output ~$0.039/image at 1K per Google table.
Batch: half price on Google pricing table.
Three SKUs; see Imagen section on Google pricing page.
Video billed per second/output unit; see Veo section on Google pricing page.
See Google models/pricing pages for music SKUs.

models.notes: See Gemini model page for pricing; specialized preview.
Agentic research; verify current pricing on Google page.
Google deprecation: shutdown June 1, 2026; migrate to 2.5+.
